title: Return Values
description: How to get a result back from a function.

slides.title: Getting Answers Back 📨
slides.content:
## Output 

Functions don't just take inputs; they can completely calculate an answer and **return** it back to whoever called the function!

To do this, you must tell Go what *type* of data the function will return, right after the parentheses.

`func getAge() int {` -> *"This function will give back an integer."*

quizData.question: Where do you specify the 'return type' of a function in Go?
quizData.explanation: The signature looks like this: `func Name(params) ReturnType { ... }`

slides.title: The 'return' Keyword 🚀
slides.content: Inside the code block, you use the `return` keyword to instantly fire the result out and stop the function.

quizData.question: If you declare `func getName() string`, but forget to include a `return` statement inside the function, Go will compile it anyway.
quizData.explanation: If you promise the compiler you will return a string, it will firmly throw an error if you fail to provide a `return` statement.
